disclaimer: Simplified ruleset for pre-flight checking only. Always verify against the current full IATA DGR / 49 CFR and applicable operator & state variations before tendering. A trained, certified shipper must sign the Shipper's Declaration.

packed_with_equipment.psn: Lithium ion batteries packed with equipment

contained_in_equipment.psn: Lithium ion batteries contained in equipment

packed_with_equipment.psn: Lithium metal batteries packed with equipment

contained_in_equipment.psn: Lithium metal batteries contained in equipment

sodium.label: Sodium-ion
sodium.note: Sodium-ion battery provisions differ from lithium. ICAO is silent on state of charge for sodium-ion with equipment (PI 977/978). This tool identifies the UN number and packing instruction; full section, quantity, and packaging classification must be verified manually against the current DGR.

packed_with_equipment.psn: Sodium ion batteries packed with equipment

contained_in_equipment.psn: Sodium ion batteries contained in equipment

rules.scope: Lithium-ion packed with / contained in equipment, cells/batteries > 2.7 Wh (new in IATA 67th ed., 2026)

stateOfCharge.note: ≤30% of rated capacity (or ≤25% indicated). Hard requirement in the 67th edition (2026), and now expanded beyond standalone UN3480 to UN3481 with equipment above 2.7 Wh.

conditions.note: Damaged, defective, recalled, and waste/for-disposal lithium batteries are forbidden for transport by air. Prototype / low-production-run batteries require competent-authority approval and ship as Section IA.

sectionII.standaloneNote: Section II was removed from PI 965 (UN3480) and PI 968 (UN3090) effective 31 March 2022. Standalone lithium cells/batteries must now ship as Section IB or IA (fully regulated).

perPackageLimits.verifyNote: Widely-published Section II per-package limits. VERIFY against the current IATA DGR before commercial reliance.

FEDEX.label: FedEx
FEDEX.note: FedEx prohibits standalone lithium cells/batteries (UN3480/UN3090) offered as Section II; must ship fully regulated and may require pre-approval.

UPS.label: UPS
UPS.note: UPS accepts hazmat on a contract basis; standalone Section II lithium may require pre-approval and computer-generated documentation.

DHL.label: DHL
DHL.note: DHL Express requires approval from its Restricted Commodities Group for standalone Section II lithium batteries.

un38_3.ref: UN Manual of Tests & Criteria, sub-section 38.3 (test summary mandatory since 1 Jan 2020)

soc_30.ref: IATA DGR 67th ed. — 30% state-of-charge limit for UN3480

soc_2026.ref: IATA DGR 67th ed. (2026) — SoC requirement expanded to UN3481 with equipment (>2.7 Wh)

iata_training.ref: IATA — DG training & certification (recurrent every 24 months)

special_provisions.ref: IATA DGR — Special Provisions for lithium batteries (A88, A99, A154, A164, A181, A213)
